danger_signals.visual_form: Hostile ships filling screen space, distinctive enemy silhouettes against background
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring evasion and counterfire

danger_signals.visual_form: Weapon level indicator dropping (visual HUD element)
danger_signals.meaning: Increasing vulnerability; ship one hit away from death at level 0

danger_signals.visual_form: Large mechanical sphere or renamed stage boss entity entering screen
danger_signals.meaning: Major threat requiring sustained combat; world-state escalation

danger_signals.visual_form: Scrolling background structures approaching screen edges
danger_signals.meaning: Crush-damage threat if caught between ship and screen boundary

danger_signals.visual_form: Increased enemy density and spawn patterns during stage progression
danger_signals.meaning: Sphere calling for backup; intensity escalation

safety_signals.visual_form: Orange or green chip items dropping from defeated enemies
safety_signals.meaning: Health recovery mechanism; weapon level-up restores survivability

safety_signals.visual_form: Special item that increases life counter display
safety_signals.meaning: Continuation checkpoint; death results in retry at last checkpoint

safety_signals.visual_form: Life counter icon becoming red when Special Lives available
safety_signals.meaning: Enhanced safety: respawn at exact death location with no progress loss
Tool flexibility; tactical advantage through weapon switching

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player-controlled ship; distinct from enemy vessel designs

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Large mechanical sphere drawing Nazca-line patterns
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Reinforcement ships called from deep space by sphere; varied enemy fighter designs
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Sphere hovers over jungle environments drawing ground patterns; draws energy from foliage
Narrative context; visual distinction indicates obsolescence vs. upgraded Super Aleste

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Earth cities under attack by mechanical sphere; sphere expanding and drawing Nazca-line patterns in jungle terrain
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Sphere activates defense systems; military attacks prove ineffective

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Increased enemy density and reinforcement wave composition; stage environments show sphere influence or destruction
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy spawning intensifies; reinforcement mechanics trigger progressively

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Named boss entity dominates screen; distinct visual presentation from regular enemies; arena-like stage layout
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss follows pattern-based attack sequences; unique vulnerability windows

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Weapon level indicator visually diminished; ship sprite may show damage state (implicit through level system)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Reduced offensive capacity; increased fragility (one hit to level 0 = death)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Extended ending sequence revealing mystery behind invasion; super-deformed art style on game-over screens
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Thi (alien co-pilot) character presence affects narrative pacing; alternate difficulty endings unlock additional lore

environmental_storytelling.example: South American jungle setting where sphere hovers, drawing Nazca-line patterns; visual metaphor for alien intelligence studying human civilization
Background structures and environments show signs of sphere-caused damage and transformation; visual progression indicates deepening into sphere's influence
Different boss encounters across stages represent sphere's expanding reach and escalating defensive measures

Eight weapon types available suggest humanity's improvised technological response to alien threat; each weapon's unique control function implies specialized design for sphere vulnerability exploitation
Japanese version includes character art (Raz and Thi) and longer ending sequence; removes mystery regarding invasion origins; visual storytelling depth tied to regional release

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Can absorb two hits before death; aggressive play possible; offensive capability maximized
Extreme fragility; defensive evasion-only play required; one hit triggers death
Spawn in waves; follow attack patterns; respond to player position; visually distinct from player ship
Displays complex attack sequences; activates defense systems; calls reinforcements; dominates visual space
Requires more chips for next level-up; diminishing return mechanic; visual progression slower at higher tiers
Instant level-up regardless of current level; highest priority visual signal for player safety
Icon turns red; signals enhanced safety tier available; respawn mechanics change behavior on death

notes: Super Aleste is a vertically-scrolling shmup where visual communication of danger/safety is tightly coupled to the weapon level system, which serves dual mechanical and narrative functions. The mechanical sphere threat is communicated through stage progression and boss design. Version differences (Japanese vs. NA/EU) include narrative divergence: Japanese version includes character-driven ending with alien co-pilot Thi and extended narrative revelation; American/European versions removed this content and altered certain music tracks. Environmental storytelling leverages the Nazca-line motif and jungle setting to establish alien intelligence thematic. The weapon-as-health mechanic creates constant visual feedback on survivability state, making the HUD weapon level indicator the primary danger/safety signal. Color-coded chips (orange vs. green) provide intuitive visual hierarchy for power-up priority.
